writeups.Name: SQL injection - File reading
writeups.Content:
# SQL injection - File reading

CTF: rootme
Category: sqli, web
Creation Time: October 7, 2021 6:53 PM
Difficulty: Easy-Medium
Last Edited: October 7, 2021 7:17 PM
Solved by: Anonymous

# Description

> Retrieve the administrator password. 

`address` : [http://challenge01.root-me.org/web-serveur/ch31/](http://challenge01.root-me.org/web-serveur/ch31/)

# Solution

![Login page](img/Filereading.png)

Login page

![Members page](img/Filereading1.png)

Members page

![When we click on admin we see some information and from the url we see the 'id=1' parameter which is vulnerable to sqli](img/Filereading2.png)

When we click on admin we see some information and from the url we see the 'id=1' parameter which is vulnerable to sqli

After playing a bit with the id parameter we discover that apexes and double quotes are escaped so we can't use them. We can use the 0x... trick to create strings from hex without using quotes

From the name of the challenge we can deduce we have to read local files. This can be done with the sql function LOAD_FILE('filename')

```
10 UNION SELECT 1,2,3,LOAD_FILE(0x2f6368616c6c656e67652f7765622d736572766575722f636833312f696e6465782e706870)
```

However trivial files like /etc/passwd won't show, probably because we don't have permission to read them. I tried writing files with SELECT .. INTO OUTFILE 'filename' but it also doesn't work

```
 10 UNION SELECT 1,2,3,4 INTO OUTFILE 0x2f746d702f6261642e747874
```

So after searching a bit I found this writeup  

[https://github.com/kahla-sec/CTF-Writeups/blob/master/Securinets Prequals 2k20/The after-Prequal/README.md](https://github.com/kahla-sec/CTF-Writeups/blob/master/Securinets%20Prequals%202k20/The%20after-Prequal/README.md)

Which, in order to understand where they can read files they use the secure_file_priv global variable

> The variable secure_file_priv is used to limit the effect of data import and export operations

So i executed this payload to dump it:

```
10 UNION SELECT 1,2,3,@@GLOBAL.secure_file_priv
```

![Untitled](img/Filereading3.png)

We obtain /challenge/web-serveur/ch31/ which is probably the location of the source files of the application which we can dump with 

```
10 UNION SELECT 1,2,3,LOAD_FILE(0x2f6368616c6c656e67652f7765622d736572766575722f636833312f696e6465782e706870)
```

![Untitled](img/Filereading4.png)

Now we have the source of the page and we can see that the users passwords are hashed with sha1 and then xor encrypted and base64 encoded. The decryption key is present in the sources so now we only need to dump the password. After enumerating the information_schema (or reading the sources) we see the table member and the field member_password. We can now obtain the admin encrypted pass:

```
10 UNION SELECT 1,2,3,member_password FROM member
```

We obtain VA5QA1cCVQgPXwEAXwZVVVsHBgtfUVBaV1QEAwIFVAJWAwBRC1tRVA==

![Untitled](img/Filereading5.png)

So now we have the sha1 of the password

![Untitled](img/Filereading6.png)

Flag is superpassword
